summary: Clear soundboard recording with brief edits; the show is solid, highlighted by a stellar Cumberland Blues and on‑point King Bee but hampered by sluggish tempos on some numbers.
review: The source captures a clean, well‑balanced mix despite a few short cuts, making it an enjoyable listen. Musically the night is decent – Cumberland Blues shines and Pigpen delivers a spot‑on King Bee, while The Other One suffers from feedback and Uncle John's Band feels overly slow. Overall the performance is respectable but not spectacular.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is moderate (average to good) while recording quality is good; weighted average yields ~3.3 stars. Only two reviews provide the data, giving medium confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Overall clear and crisp sound; brief edit cuts are present but unobtrusive. No major drop‑outs reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A generally mellow set tempered by feedback on The Other One and an experimental, slower Uncle John's Band that slows the momentum; high points keep the show engaging.
